# Owner WhatsApp Interface — Implementation Prompts

This file contains five sequential implementation slices. Run them in order. Each slice
is self-contained and includes its own inspection, architecture, and definition-of-done
instructions. Do not start a later slice until the earlier one passes its DoD.

---

## Context for all slices

**What this feature is:**
A WhatsApp-first interface for property owners who do not want to log into a portal for
routine interactions. Owners receive proactive pushes (approvals, job completions,
vacancies, monthly digest) and can take structured actions (YES/NO approvals, confirmed
via one-time challenge code) entirely within WhatsApp. The web portal remains the
fallback for complex interactions (reports, cashflow plans, RFP details).

**Design philosophy — push + structured reply, not open conversation:**
Unlike the tenant interface, owners do not need an AI conversation loop. They need:
1. To be notified of the right things at the right time.
2. To be able to act on those notifications without opening a browser.

The owner WhatsApp interface is therefore **mostly outbound push + structured inbound
reply** (YES/NO/APPROVE + challenge code). No Claude API is needed for v1. The inbox
handler is a finite-state machine, not an LLM.

**Slice priority — maximum value first:**
1. **Slice 1 (phone binding):** Identity prerequisite — nothing else works without it.
2. **Slice 2 (approvals):** Highest-friction owner pain point. Owners delay maintenance
   approvals because logging in feels costly. One-reply approval eliminates this.
3. **Slice 3 (operational notifications):** Keeps owners informed without portal logins —
   job completions, vacancies, invoice receipts.
4. **Slice 4 (monthly digest):** Replaces the need to open the reporting tab for most
   owners. Delivers the net position as a single WhatsApp message.
5. **Slice 5 (cashflow plan approval):** High-stakes, less frequent — gated on the
   cashflow planning epic being adopted.

**Infrastructure dependency:**
This epic requires the `WhatsAppOutbox` table and drain job from the tenant multichannel
epic (Slice 3 of `tenant-multichannel.md`). If that slice has not been completed, Slice 1
of this epic must create the `WhatsAppOutbox` model, `whatsAppOutboxRepository.ts`, and
`whatsAppService.ts` first — using the exact same schema and patterns documented in that
slice. Do not build a parallel outbox. One outbox serves both tenants and owners.

**Key design decisions:**
- **Identity binding:** Owners are `User` records with JWT auth. They have no phone in
  the current schema. Slice 1 adds `phone` + `phoneVerifiedAt` to `User` and a binding
  flow (OTP via WhatsApp). Phone lookup drives all inbound webhook handling.
- **Security for approvals:** Any approval action via WhatsApp requires a one-time
  challenge code sent back to the same number. This prevents spoofing and creates an
  audit trail. The challenge is always sent — there is no cost threshold below which it
  is skipped. Keep it simple: one consistent rule.
- **Inbound state machine:** The Twilio webhook handler for owners is a lightweight FSM.
  States: `IDLE` → `AWAITING_CHALLENGE` → `IDLE`. No conversation history, no LLM.
- **Audit trail:** Every owner WhatsApp action (approval, rejection) is stamped with
  `channel: WHATSAPP`, `fromPhone`, `confirmedAt` on the relevant workflow record.
- **Portal deep links:** Notifications that are information-only (job completed, vacancy)
  carry a short deep link to the relevant portal page. Links are signed if sensitive.
- **Monthly digest:** Generated by a cron job on the first of each month. Pulls from
  `BuildingFinancialSnapshot`, open `MaintenanceRequest` count, and upcoming lease
  expirations. Per-building if owner owns multiple buildings; portfolio rollup appended.
- **WhatsApp provider:** Twilio Messaging API (same as tenant multichannel).
- **No Voice/IVR for owners:** Owners interact asynchronously — Voice adds complexity
  with minimal benefit for this persona. Explicitly out of scope for all slices.

**Out of scope for all slices:**
- Open-ended AI conversation (that is the tenant interface).
- Voice/IVR channel.
- WhatsApp template message pre-approval (for production Twilio deployment, templates
  must be submitted to Meta — this is a procurement/ops task, not a code task).
- Multi-language owner notifications (English only for v1; same rule as F-UI7).
- Owner-initiated outbound messages (owner texting first to ask a question) — v1 is
  push-driven; inbound is only for replies to system-initiated threads.

---

## Slice 1 of 5 — Owner phone binding and WhatsApp identity

Read `PROJECT_OVERVIEW.md` first (entry point), then
`apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md` (lookup), then `PROJECT_STATE.md`
(canonical reference), `docs/AUDIT.md`, and `apps/api/blueprint.js`. Obey all guardrails
exactly. Preserve existing behaviour unless explicitly required for this slice.

### Slice name: `owner-phone-binding`

**Goal:** Add phone number binding to the `User` model (migration), a portal settings
page where an owner can link their WhatsApp number via OTP verification, and the backend
endpoints that drive the binding flow. This is the identity prerequisite for all
subsequent slices.

Also verify — or create if absent — the `WhatsAppOutbox` model, repository, and service
from tenant multichannel Slice 3. If that infrastructure already exists, skip re-creating
it and note the dependency.

### Before writing code

1. Read `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`. Record:
   - The exact `User` model fields. Note whether `phone` exists already.
   - Whether `WhatsAppOutbox` and `OutboxStatus` already exist (from tenant multichannel).
   - The `Building` model — specifically how owner↔building ownership is modelled.
2. Read `apps/api/src/routes/` to find the existing owner portal routes (look for routes
   using `requireRole(req, res, "OWNER")`). Understand the auth pattern owners use.
3. Read `apps/api/src/authz.ts`. Understand `getAuthUser` and how `userId` is extracted
   from the JWT — this is how owner identity is resolved in all new endpoints.
4. Read `apps/web/pages/manager/people/` or similar to find if there is an existing owner
   profile/settings page. The binding UI goes either there or in a new
   `apps/web/pages/owner/settings.js` if no owner settings page exists.
5. Check `apps/api/package.json` for the `twilio` SDK. If absent, note it must be added.
6. Output a short implementation plan before writing any code:
   - Whether WhatsApp infrastructure needs to be created or reused.
   - Where the owner settings UI will live.
   - The exact schema additions required.

### Architecture rules

- The OTP flow uses WhatsApp itself for delivery (not SMS). The Twilio WhatsApp
  sandbox/number sends the code; the owner enters it in the portal UI. This means
  `whatsAppService.sendMessage` must be available before the binding flow works.
- Never store the raw OTP in the `User` record permanently. Use three nullable fields
  (`pendingPhone`, `phoneOtp`, `phoneOtpExpiresAt`) that are cleared on successful
  verification or expiry.
- OTP is 6 digits, expires in 10 minutes. Generate with `crypto.randomInt(100000, 999999)`.
- Routes thin, Prisma in repositories only (G9). OTP generation and verification logic in
  `ownerNotificationService.ts` (new, reused in later slices).
- The binding endpoint is owner-only (`requireRole(req, res, "OWNER")`).
- Phone must be stored in E.164 format. Validate with a simple regex before persisting.

### Schema additions

Add to `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` (on the `User` model):

```prisma
// On User model — add these fields:
phone             String?    // E.164 format, e.g. +41791234567
phoneVerifiedAt   DateTime?
pendingPhone      String?    // Phone being verified (cleared on success/expiry)
phoneOtp          String?    // Hashed OTP (cleared on success/expiry)
phoneOtpExpiresAt DateTime?
```

If `WhatsAppOutbox` does not exist yet, also add (copying exactly from
`tenant-multichannel.md` Slice 3 schema):

```prisma
model WhatsAppOutbox {
  id           String       @id @default(cuid())
  orgId        String
  toPhone      String
  body         String       @db.Text
  status       OutboxStatus @default(PENDING)
  retryCount   Int          @default(0)
  errorMessage String?
  createdAt    DateTime     @default(now())
  sentAt       DateTime?

  @@index([status, createdAt])
}

enum OutboxStatus {
  PENDING
  SENT
  FAILED
}
```

Generate and apply the migration:
```bash
cd apps/api && npx prisma migrate dev --name add_owner_phone_binding
```

### New files — in this order

1. `apps/api/src/repositories/ownerRepository.ts` (or extend if it exists)
   - `findUserById(prisma, userId)` — for OTP verification lookup.
   - `setOwnerPhone(prisma, userId, phone)` — clears OTP fields, sets `phone` +
     `phoneVerifiedAt`.
   - `setPendingOtp(prisma, userId, pendingPhone, hashedOtp, expiresAt)`.
   - `clearOtpFields(prisma, userId)`.

2. `apps/api/src/repositories/whatsAppOutboxRepository.ts` *(skip if already exists)*
   - `enqueue(prisma, orgId, toPhone, body)`.
   - `dequeuePending(prisma, limit)` — PENDING + retryCount < 3, oldest first.
   - `markSent(prisma, id)`, `markFailed(prisma, id, errorMessage)`, `incrementRetry(prisma, id)`.

3. `apps/api/src/services/whatsAppService.ts` *(skip if already exists)*
   - `sendMessage(toPhone, body)` — calls Twilio REST API.
   - `drainOutbox(prisma)` — fetches pending, sends, updates status.

4. `apps/api/src/services/ownerNotificationService.ts`
   - `startPhoneBinding(prisma, userId, rawPhone)`:
     - Validate E.164 format.
     - Generate 6-digit OTP, hash with `bcrypt` (cost 10).
     - Call `ownerRepository.setPendingOtp`.
     - Enqueue WhatsApp message: "Your Maintenance Agent verification code is: [OTP].
       It expires in 10 minutes."
     - Return `{ enqueued: true }`.
   - `confirmPhoneBinding(prisma, userId, enteredOtp)`:
     - Load user, check `phoneOtpExpiresAt` not passed.
     - Compare `enteredOtp` against stored hash.
     - On match: call `ownerRepository.setOwnerPhone`, return `{ verified: true }`.
     - On mismatch: return `{ verified: false, reason: "INVALID_OTP" }`.
     - On expiry: clear OTP fields, return `{ verified: false, reason: "EXPIRED" }`.

5. `apps/api/src/routes/ownerPhone.ts`
   - `POST /owner/phone/start` — `requireRole("OWNER")`. Body: `{ phone }`. Calls
     `ownerNotificationService.startPhoneBinding`. Returns `{ data: { enqueued: true } }`.
   - `POST /owner/phone/confirm` — `requireRole("OWNER")`. Body: `{ otp }`. Calls
     `ownerNotificationService.confirmPhoneBinding`. Returns verified status.
   - `DELETE /owner/phone` — `requireRole("OWNER")`. Clears `phone` + `phoneVerifiedAt`.
     Used if owner wants to unbind or change number.
   - Register in main router.

6. Background job extension — register `whatsAppService.drainOutbox` on a 30-second
   interval (same job runner pattern as existing background jobs; create
   `apps/api/src/jobs/whatsAppOutboxJob.ts` if no job runner exists).

### New frontend files — in this order

1. `apps/web/pages/api/owner/phone/start.js` — proxy POST to `/owner/phone/start`.
2. `apps/web/pages/api/owner/phone/confirm.js` — proxy POST to `/owner/phone/confirm`.
3. `apps/web/pages/api/owner/phone/index.js` — proxy DELETE to `/owner/phone`.
4. `apps/web/pages/owner/settings.js` (or add a Settings tab to the existing owner
   dashboard if one exists):
   - Panel: "WhatsApp Notifications".
   - If no phone bound: phone input field + "Send verification code" button.
   - After sending: OTP input field + "Verify" button + 10-minute countdown.
   - If phone bound: show masked number (e.g. `+41 79 *** ** 67`) + "Remove" button.
   - Follow F-UI2 (detail page layout), F-UI4 (no inline styles).

### Files to modify

- `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` — schema additions above.
- `apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md` — add `ownerPhone.ts` to route index;
  add `ownerRepository.ts` + `whatsAppOutboxRepository.ts` to repo index; add
  `ownerNotificationService.ts` + `whatsAppService.ts` to service index. Update counts.
- `SCHEMA_REFERENCE.md` — add `WhatsAppOutbox` (if new) + `OutboxStatus`; document new
  `User` fields; update model/enum counts.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — claim port 3224 for `ownerPhone.test.ts`; update next available
  to 3225. (Adjust if ports 3224+ are already claimed by tenant-multichannel slices.)
- `PROJECT_STATE.md` — update Document Integrity table counts.
- `.env.example` — add `TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID`, `TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN`, `TWILIO_WHATSAPP_FROM`
  if not already present.
- `apps/api/package.json` — add `twilio` + `bcrypt` + `@types/bcrypt` if not present.
- `docs/FRONTEND_INVENTORY.md` — add new pages/proxies, update counts.
- `apps/api/openapi.yaml` — add 3 phone binding endpoints.
- `apps/api/src/__tests__/contracts.test.ts` — add contract tests for the 3 new endpoints.

### Auth notes

All three phone endpoints use `requireRole(req, res, "OWNER")` — standard JWT auth via
`withAuthRequired`. The `userId` comes from `getAuthUser(req)?.userId`. Never accept
`userId` as a body parameter.

### In scope
- `phone` + OTP fields on `User` model (migration).
- OTP binding flow: start (send code via WhatsApp) + confirm (verify + activate).
- WhatsApp outbox infrastructure (if not already built by tenant multichannel).
- Owner settings UI with binding widget.

### Out of scope
- Inbound WhatsApp replies from owners (Slice 2).
- Approval notifications (Slice 2).
- Any notification content beyond the OTP verification message.

### Definition of done

- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit --project apps/api/tsconfig.json` — zero errors.
- [ ] `npm test --prefix apps/api` — all suites pass.
- [ ] `POST /owner/phone/start` with a valid owner token and E.164 phone enqueues a
  `WhatsAppOutbox` record with the OTP message body.
- [ ] `POST /owner/phone/confirm` with the correct OTP sets `phone` + `phoneVerifiedAt`
  on the `User` record.
- [ ] Portal settings page renders the binding widget and flows through start → confirm.
- [ ] Blueprint doc sync: `node apps/api/blueprint.js` exits clean.

## Slice 2 of 5 — Maintenance request approval via WhatsApp

Read `PROJECT_OVERVIEW.md` first, then `apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md`,
then `PROJECT_STATE.md`. Slice 1 must be complete and green before starting.

### Slice name: `owner-whatsapp-approvals`

**Goal:** When a maintenance request reaches `AWAITING_OWNER_APPROVAL`, send the owner a
WhatsApp message with a YES/NO prompt. The Twilio inbound webhook handles the reply,
issues a challenge code, and on confirmation executes the approval or rejection through
the existing `ownerDirect` workflow. Every action is stamped with channel + phone +
timestamp for the audit trail.

This slice delivers the highest owner value: decisions that currently require a portal
login can be made from a single WhatsApp reply.

### Before writing code

1. Read `apps/api/src/workflows/` — find the owner-direct workflow (likely
   `ownerDirectWorkflow.ts` or similar). Record:
   - The exact function signatures for `approveRequest` and `rejectRequest`.
   - What parameters they expect (`orgId`, `requestId`, `userId`, etc.).
   - What domain events they emit.
2. Read `apps/api/src/routes/` — find where `AWAITING_OWNER_APPROVAL` status transitions
   are triggered. Understand what data is available at the point of notification (request
   id, description, estimated cost, contractor name).
3. Read `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`. Confirm the `MaintenanceRequest` model has a
   field linking it to the responsible owner (via building → owner FK chain). Trace the
   exact path: `MaintenanceRequest → Unit → Building → owner (User)`.
4. Read `apps/api/src/services/ownerNotificationService.ts` (from Slice 1). You will
   extend this file with approval notification methods.
5. Understand the Twilio inbound webhook shape: `From`, `Body`, `To` in the POST body.
   The owner WhatsApp webhook is separate from any tenant webhook — use a distinct path.

### The approval FSM

The inbound webhook for owner WhatsApp replies is a simple two-state machine per owner:

```
IDLE
  ↓ (owner replies YES or NO to an approval message)
AWAITING_CHALLENGE
  - System sends a 6-digit challenge code to the owner's WhatsApp
  - Record: { userId, requestId, action: "APPROVE"|"REJECT", code, expiresAt }
  ↓ (owner replies with the 6-digit code within 10 minutes)
IDLE
  - Execute the action (approve or reject)
  - Send confirmation message
  - Clear pending challenge record
```

If the owner replies with anything other than YES/NO in IDLE state, reply with a
polite "I didn't understand that. If you're trying to approve or reject a maintenance
request, please use the link in the notification message."

If the owner takes longer than 10 minutes to reply with the code, the challenge expires,
the pending record is deleted, and the system replies: "Your confirmation code has
expired. Please log in to the portal to take action: [link]."

### Schema additions

```prisma
model OwnerWhatsAppChallenge {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId     String   @unique   // one pending challenge per owner at a time
  requestId  String?            // null for cashflow plan challenges (Slice 5)
  action     String             // "APPROVE" or "REJECT"
  code       String             // 6-digit code (plain — short-lived, not a password)
  expiresAt  DateTime
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())

  user    User               @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  request MaintenanceRequest? @relation(fields: [requestId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
}
```

Generate and apply the migration:
```bash
cd apps/api && npx prisma migrate dev --name add_owner_whatsapp_challenge
```

### New files — in this order

1. `apps/api/src/repositories/ownerWhatsAppChallengeRepository.ts`
   - `createChallenge(prisma, userId, requestId, action, code, expiresAt)` — upserts
     (one challenge per owner; replaces any existing one).
   - `findPendingChallenge(prisma, userId)` — returns challenge if not expired.
   - `deleteChallenge(prisma, userId)`.

2. Extend `apps/api/src/services/ownerNotificationService.ts`:
   - `notifyApprovalRequired(prisma, requestId)`:
     - Resolve request → unit → building → owner `User`. If owner has no verified phone,
       log a warning and return (silent fail — portal notification still exists).
     - Format message: "Maintenance request at [building name]: [description]. Estimated
       cost: CHF [amount]. Contractor: [name]. Reply YES to approve or NO to reject."
     - Enqueue via `whatsAppOutboxRepository.enqueue`.
   - `handleInboundOwnerMessage(prisma, fromPhone, messageBody)`:
     - Look up `User` by `phone = fromPhone` + `phoneVerifiedAt IS NOT NULL`.
     - If not found: reply "I don't recognise this number…", return.
     - Check `ownerWhatsAppChallengeRepository.findPendingChallenge`:
       - **If pending challenge exists and body matches code:** resolve the action (call
         `approveRequestWorkflow` or `rejectRequestWorkflow`), delete challenge, send
         confirmation message.
       - **If pending challenge exists but code mismatch:** reply "That code is incorrect.
         Please try again or visit the portal: [link]."
       - **If no pending challenge and body is YES/NO (case-insensitive):** find the most
         recent open `OwnerWhatsAppChallenge`-eligible request for this owner (status
         `AWAITING_OWNER_APPROVAL`). If found: generate 6-digit code, create challenge
         record, send challenge message. If not found: reply "You don't have any
         maintenance requests awaiting your approval right now."
       - **Anything else:** reply with the "I didn't understand" message.

3. `apps/api/src/routes/ownerWhatsAppWebhook.ts`
   - `POST /webhooks/owner/whatsapp` — Twilio webhook, no session auth, Twilio signature
     validation (skip in `NODE_ENV === 'test'`).
   - Extracts `From` (strip `whatsapp:` prefix) and `Body`.
   - Calls `ownerNotificationService.handleInboundOwnerMessage`.
   - Returns empty `<Response/>` TwiML (replies are asynchronous via outbox).
   - Register in main router.

4. Wire the domain event that fires `notifyApprovalRequired`:
   - Find where `REQUEST_STATUS_CHANGED` (or equivalent) is emitted when status becomes
     `AWAITING_OWNER_APPROVAL`. In that event listener, call
     `ownerNotificationService.notifyApprovalRequired(prisma, requestId)`.
   - If no suitable event exists, add the emit to the relevant workflow (one atomic
     commit: workflow change + listener).

### Files to modify

- `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` — schema addition above.
- `apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md` — update counts.
- `SCHEMA_REFERENCE.md` — add `OwnerWhatsAppChallenge`; update model count.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — claim port 3225 for `ownerWhatsAppApprovals.test.ts`; update next
  available. (Adjust numbering based on what tenant-multichannel has already claimed.)
- `PROJECT_STATE.md` — update Document Integrity table.
- `apps/api/openapi.yaml` — add `POST /webhooks/owner/whatsapp` (Twilio-facing, not
  owner-auth-facing).

### Auth notes

The owner WhatsApp webhook (`/webhooks/owner/whatsapp`) uses Twilio signature validation,
not JWT. Owner identity is resolved from the `User.phone` field, not from a JWT. The
Twilio middleware is the same helper as built for tenant/voice webhooks — reuse it.

The approval execution (`approveRequestWorkflow`, `rejectRequestWorkflow`) still requires
a `userId` — pass the resolved owner's `userId`. The audit trail records
`channel: WHATSAPP`.

### In scope
- `OwnerWhatsAppChallenge` model + migration.
- `notifyApprovalRequired` (push notification on status change).
- Inbound YES/NO → challenge → code confirm → workflow execution FSM.
- Audit stamp (channel, phone, confirmedAt) on approval/rejection.

### Out of scope
- Job completion / vacancy notifications (Slice 3).
- Cashflow plan approval (Slice 5).
- Any approval action other than `APPROVE` / `REJECT` on maintenance requests.

### Definition of done

- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit --project apps/api/tsconfig.json` — zero errors.
- [ ] `npm test --prefix apps/api` — all suites pass.
- [ ] Integration test: transition a request to `AWAITING_OWNER_APPROVAL` → verify
  `WhatsAppOutbox` record created with correct owner phone and message body.
- [ ] Integration test: simulate `YES` reply → challenge created → correct code reply →
  request status advances to `OWNER_APPROVED`.
- [ ] Integration test: simulate `NO` reply → challenge created → correct code reply →
  request status advances to `OWNER_REJECTED`.
- [ ] Integration test: expired challenge → system replies with expiry message, no
  status change.
- [ ] Blueprint doc sync exits clean.

## Slice 3 of 5 — Operational event notifications

Read `PROJECT_OVERVIEW.md` first, then `apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md`,
then `PROJECT_STATE.md`. Slices 1 and 2 must be complete and green before starting.

### Slice name: `owner-whatsapp-notifications`

**Goal:** Send owners proactive WhatsApp push notifications for operational events they
currently discover only by logging into the portal: job completions, new vacancies, and
significant invoice receipts. All are push-only — no inbound reply expected. Include a
deep link to the relevant portal page in every message.

These notifications collectively replace the owner's need to check the portal for routine
updates. The portal remains the destination for detail; WhatsApp is the nudge.

### Before writing code

1. Read `apps/api/src/workflows/` — find workflows that emit events for:
   - Job status change to `COMPLETED`.
   - Lease termination or unit status change indicating a vacancy.
   - Invoice approval or `ISSUED` status (for significant invoices).
   Record the exact event names, their payloads, and what data is available at emit time.
2. Read `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`. Trace the FK paths:
   - `Job → MaintenanceRequest → Unit → Building → owner (User)`.
   - `Lease → Unit → Building → owner (User)`.
   - `Invoice → Job → MaintenanceRequest → Unit → Building → owner (User)` (or direct
     building FK if it exists).
3. Read `apps/api/src/services/ownerNotificationService.ts` (from Slices 1–2). You will
   add new notification methods to this file.
4. Check whether a `Building.ownerId` or `Building.ownerUserId` FK exists directly on
   the `Building` model. If it does, use it to resolve the owner. If not, trace the chain
   from G9-compliant repository methods.

### Architecture rules

- All notification methods follow the same pattern: resolve owner → check verified phone
  → if absent, log warning and return (silent fail) → enqueue WhatsApp message.
- Never resolve the owner inside the domain event listener. The listener calls the
  notification service method with `prisma + <entity>Id`; the service resolves the rest.
- Message format: short, plain text, ends with a portal deep link. No markdown, no
  bullet lists — WhatsApp renders these inconsistently.
- Deep links use `process.env.APP_BASE_URL` (add to `.env.example` if absent). If the
  env var is not set, omit the link gracefully rather than crashing.

### Notification events (v1)

| Trigger | Message template |
|---|---|
| Job → `COMPLETED` | "Work completed at [building name]: [request description] ([contractor name]). Invoice: CHF [amount]. View details: [link]" |
| Unit becomes vacant (lease ends or terminated) | "Unit [unit ref] at [building name] is now vacant (available from [date]). View listings: [link]" |
| Invoice > CHF 1,000 moves to `ISSUED` | "Invoice received: CHF [amount] from [contractor/vendor] for [building name]. View invoice: [link]" |

The CHF 1,000 threshold for invoice notifications is a hardcoded constant in
`ownerNotificationService.ts` (named `OWNER_INVOICE_NOTIFICATION_THRESHOLD_CENTS`).
Add a comment noting this should become a per-owner preference in a future epic.

### Files to modify / extend

- `apps/api/src/services/ownerNotificationService.ts` — add three notification methods:
  `notifyJobCompleted(prisma, jobId)`, `notifyUnitVacant(prisma, unitId, vacantFrom)`,
  `notifyInvoiceIssued(prisma, invoiceId)`.
- Domain event listeners file — register the three new listeners.
- If the event that fires on vacancy does not exist: add the domain event emit to the
  relevant workflow (lease termination or unit status change). One atomic commit.
- `apps/api/src/services/notificationTemplates.ts` — add owner notification templates
  (or create the file if tenant-multichannel Slice 5 has not been run yet).
- `.env.example` — add `APP_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000`.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — claim next available port for `ownerNotifications.test.ts`.
- `PROJECT_STATE.md` — update Document Integrity table.

### Auth notes

Push-only. No inbound webhook handling in this slice. No new auth surface.

### In scope
- Job completion, vacancy, and large invoice push notifications.
- Deep links to portal pages.
- `APP_BASE_URL` env var for link generation.
- Domain event listeners for all three triggers.

### Out of scope
- RFP outcome notifications (quote received, contractor selected) — lower owner priority,
  add to backlog.
- Invoice notifications below CHF 1,000.
- Any inbound reply handling for these notifications.

### Definition of done

- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit --project apps/api/tsconfig.json` — zero errors.
- [ ] `npm test --prefix apps/api` — all suites pass.
- [ ] Integration test: complete a job via workflow → verify `WhatsAppOutbox` record
  created for the building owner with correct phone and message body.
- [ ] Integration test: terminate a lease → verify vacancy notification enqueued.
- [ ] Integration test: invoice at CHF 500 issued → no WhatsApp record. Invoice at
  CHF 1,500 issued → WhatsApp record created.
- [ ] Blueprint doc sync exits clean.

---

## Slice 4 of 5 — Monthly portfolio digest

Read `PROJECT_OVERVIEW.md` first, then `apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md`,
then `PROJECT_STATE.md`. Slices 1–3 must be complete and green before starting.

### Slice name: `owner-whatsapp-digest`

**Goal:** On the first day of each month, send every owner with a verified WhatsApp
number a concise text digest of the prior month: net position, open requests, and any
upcoming lease expirations. For owners with multiple buildings, send one message per
building followed by a portfolio rollup. This replaces the portal reporting tab for
owners who only need the headline numbers.

### Before writing code

1. Read `apps/api/src/services/financialsService.ts` (or wherever
   `BuildingFinancialSnapshot` is populated). Understand:
   - Which fields give `earnedIncomeCents`, `expensesTotalCents`, and `capexTotalCents`
     for a given building + month.
   - Whether `projectedIncomeCents` is available for the prior month.
2. Read `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`. Understand:
   - How a `User` (owner) is linked to their buildings. Is there a direct
     `Building.ownerId` FK? Or via a join table?
   - The `Lease` model — specifically `endDate` and `status` fields for upcoming
     expiration detection.
3. Read `apps/api/src/__tests__/` to understand how cron/scheduled jobs are tested in
   this codebase (look for any existing job test pattern). If none exists, the test will
   mock the scheduler and call the job function directly.
4. Check whether a cron/scheduler already exists in `apps/api/src/jobs/` or `src/server.ts`.
   Understand how to add a new monthly job to whatever mechanism is already in use.

### Architecture rules

- The digest job is a pure function: `generateAndSendDigests(prisma)`. It must be
  independently callable (not coupled to a specific cron library call) so it can be
  unit-tested without mocking a scheduler.
- It runs on the 1st of each month at 07:00 local time (Europe/Zurich). Use the
  existing job runner; if none exists, add a lightweight cron via `node-cron`.
- Financial data comes from `BuildingFinancialSnapshot` for the **prior calendar month**.
  If no snapshot exists for a building for the prior month, omit the financial line from
  that building's digest and add "Financials not yet available for this period." Do not
  crash or skip the entire digest.
- Message length: WhatsApp renders up to ~1000 characters comfortably. Keep each
  per-building block under 400 characters; portfolio rollup under 200 characters.
- Send via `WhatsAppOutbox` (not direct Twilio API call) so the drain job handles
  retries.

### Message format

Per building:
```
[Building name] — [Month] [Year]
Income: CHF [amount]
Expenses: CHF [amount]
Net: CHF [amount]
Open requests: [n]
Upcoming lease expirations: [n] (next 90 days)
[link to building reporting page]
```

Portfolio rollup (only if owner has > 1 building):
```
Portfolio total — [Month] [Year]
Income: CHF [amount] | Expenses: CHF [amount] | Net: CHF [amount]
[link to portfolio overview]
```

### New files — in this order

1. `apps/api/src/services/ownerDigestService.ts`
   - `buildBuildingDigest(prisma, building, priorMonth)` → formatted string.
   - `buildPortfolioRollup(buildingDigests[])` → formatted string.
   - `generateAndSendDigests(prisma)`:
     - Find all `User` records with `phoneVerifiedAt IS NOT NULL` and `role = OWNER`.
     - For each owner, resolve their buildings.
     - For each building, call `buildBuildingDigest`.
     - If > 1 building, append portfolio rollup.
     - Enqueue via `whatsAppOutboxRepository.enqueue` (one `WhatsAppOutbox` record per
       message block — do not concatenate all buildings into one message).

2. `apps/api/src/jobs/ownerDigestJob.ts`
   - Schedules `ownerDigestService.generateAndSendDigests(prisma)` on cron
     `0 7 1 * *` (07:00 on the 1st of each month, Europe/Zurich).
   - Import and register in the server startup file alongside other jobs.

### Files to modify

- Server startup file (`src/server.ts` or `src/jobs/index.ts`) — register
  `ownerDigestJob`.
- `apps/api/package.json` — add `node-cron` + `@types/node-cron` if not already present.
- `apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md` — add `ownerDigestService.ts` to
  service index; update counts.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — claim next available port for `ownerDigest.test.ts`.
- `PROJECT_STATE.md` — update Document Integrity table.

### Auth notes

The digest job runs server-side on a schedule. No HTTP auth surface. The job uses
`prisma` directly (passed from the server context); it is not triggered by any HTTP
request.

### In scope
- Per-building digest message generation.
- Portfolio rollup for multi-building owners.
- Monthly cron job (07:00 Europe/Zurich, 1st of month).
- Graceful handling of missing `BuildingFinancialSnapshot` data.

### Out of scope
- On-demand digest (owner requesting a digest mid-month via WhatsApp reply) — add to
  backlog.
- Digest delivery tracking (confirmation that the owner read it).
- Customisable digest frequency per owner.

### Definition of done

- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit --project apps/api/tsconfig.json` — zero errors.
- [ ] `npm test --prefix apps/api` — all suites pass.
- [ ] Unit test: `buildBuildingDigest` with mocked snapshot data produces correctly
  formatted string with all fields.
- [ ] Unit test: `buildBuildingDigest` with no snapshot data produces the "not yet
  available" fallback string.
- [ ] Integration test: `generateAndSendDigests` with two buildings owned by one owner
  enqueues two per-building messages + one rollup message.
- [ ] Cron schedule string `0 7 1 * *` is present in `ownerDigestJob.ts`.
- [ ] Blueprint doc sync exits clean.

---

## Slice 5 of 5 — Cashflow plan approval via WhatsApp

Read `PROJECT_OVERVIEW.md` first, then `apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md`,
then `PROJECT_STATE.md`. Slices 1–4 must be complete and green. The cashflow planning
epic (`cashflow-planning.md`) must also be complete — specifically the `CashflowPlan`
model and `approvePlanWorkflow` must exist.

### Slice name: `owner-whatsapp-cashflow`

**Goal:** When a cashflow plan is submitted for approval (`SUBMITTED` status), notify the
owner via WhatsApp with a summary and a deep link. The owner can reply APPROVE; the
system issues a challenge code; on confirmation the plan transitions to `APPROVED`. This
reuses the `OwnerWhatsAppChallenge` model and FSM from Slice 2 — extended to handle
`cashflowPlanId` in addition to `requestId`.

### Before writing code

1. Read `apps/api/src/workflows/cashflowPlanWorkflow.ts`. Understand:
   - What happens in `submitPlanWorkflow` and `approvePlanWorkflow`.
   - What domain event (if any) is emitted on `SUBMITTED`.
   - What parameters `approvePlanWorkflow` expects.
2. Read `apps/api/src/routes/cashflowPlans.ts`. Understand the approval endpoint
   (`POST /cashflow-plans/:id/approve`) to confirm you will call the workflow correctly
   from the WhatsApp handler.
3. Read `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma`. Confirm `OwnerWhatsAppChallenge` already has
   `requestId String?` as nullable (from Slice 2). You will use `cashflowPlanId String?`
   in parallel. If the schema has `requestId` as non-nullable, that is a Slice 2 bug —
   fix it with a migration before continuing.
4. Read `apps/api/src/services/ownerNotificationService.ts`. The inbound FSM handler
   already handles YES → challenge → code → approve/reject for requests. You will extend
   it to also match `cashflowPlanId` in the challenge record.

### Schema additions

Extend `OwnerWhatsAppChallenge` (already from Slice 2):

```prisma
// Add to OwnerWhatsAppChallenge:
cashflowPlanId  String?

cashflowPlan CashflowPlan? @relation(fields: [cashflowPlanId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
```

Also add back-relation on `CashflowPlan`:

```prisma
// Add to CashflowPlan:
ownerChallenges OwnerWhatsAppChallenge[]
```

Generate and apply the migration:
```bash
cd apps/api && npx prisma migrate dev --name add_owner_challenge_cashflow_plan
```

### Changes to existing files — in this order

1. `apps/api/src/repositories/ownerWhatsAppChallengeRepository.ts` — update
   `createChallenge` signature to accept `cashflowPlanId?: string` alongside the
   existing `requestId?: string`. Ensure exactly one of `requestId` / `cashflowPlanId`
   is non-null per record.

2. Extend `apps/api/src/services/ownerNotificationService.ts`:
   - Add `notifyCashflowPlanSubmitted(prisma, planId)`:
     - Resolve `CashflowPlan → orgId → owner` (how owner↔plan is linked depends on
       whether `CashflowPlan` has a direct `ownerId` or is resolved via the building FK).
       Read the schema before assuming.
     - Format message: "Cashflow plan '[plan name]' for [building name] covering
       [horizon] months has been submitted for your approval. Estimated CapEx:
       CHF [total]. Reply APPROVE to approve. View full plan: [link]"
     - Enqueue via outbox.
   - Extend `handleInboundOwnerMessage` — in the YES/APPROVE branch:
     - After checking for a pending challenge, also check for recently submitted
       cashflow plans awaiting the owner's approval (status `SUBMITTED`).
     - On challenge confirmation where `cashflowPlanId` is set: call
       `approvePlanWorkflow` with the resolved `orgId`, `planId`, and `actorUserId`.

3. Wire the domain event: when a cashflow plan moves to `SUBMITTED`, call
   `ownerNotificationService.notifyCashflowPlanSubmitted`. Add the event emit to
   `submitPlanWorkflow` if not already emitted.

### Files to modify

- `apps/api/prisma/schema.prisma` — schema additions above.
- `apps/api/src/ARCHITECTURE_LOW_CONTEXT_GUIDE.md` — update counts if any new files.
- `SCHEMA_REFERENCE.md` — update `OwnerWhatsAppChallenge` entry to note `cashflowPlanId`.
- `CONTRIBUTING.md` — claim next available port for `ownerCashflowApproval.test.ts`.
- `PROJECT_STATE.md` — update Document Integrity table.

### Auth notes

Same as Slice 2. Owner identity from `User.phone`; action execution uses resolved
`userId`. `approvePlanWorkflow` must be called with a valid `orgId` — resolve from the
plan record, not from the WhatsApp message.

### In scope
- Cashflow plan `SUBMITTED` push notification.
- APPROVE reply → challenge → confirm → `APPROVED` via `approvePlanWorkflow`.
- Extension of `OwnerWhatsAppChallenge` to carry `cashflowPlanId`.

### Out of scope
- Rejection of cashflow plans via WhatsApp (managers revise and resubmit; owner simply
  doesn't reply if they want to discuss — add explicit rejection to backlog).
- Plan editing or override changes via WhatsApp.
- Notification to manager when owner approves (portal already reflects status change).

### Definition of done

- [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit --project apps/api/tsconfig.json` — zero errors.
- [ ] `npm test --prefix apps/api` — all suites pass.
- [ ] Integration test: submit a cashflow plan → verify `WhatsAppOutbox` record created
  for the building owner with plan name and estimated CapEx in the message body.
- [ ] Integration test: simulate `APPROVE` WhatsApp reply → challenge issued → correct
  code reply → plan status advances to `APPROVED`.
- [ ] Integration test: challenge expires → system replies with expiry message, plan
  status unchanged.
- [ ] Blueprint doc sync exits clean.
